parsed.clarification_question: Which focus do you want for this section? Choose one: (A) sentence-level editing for clarity/conciseness (R1.6); (B) restructuring/reframing the contribution and claims at the section level (R1.1, R1.5); (C) checking logical consistency and whether claims align with the stated experiments (R1.2, R1.3); or (D) assessing novelty vs prior work and suggesting citations/positioning (R1.4, R1.7).

rationale.RX.1: The request is underspecified, so we must surface uncertainty about the intended assistance and any assumptions in the projection.
rationale.RX.2: We should avoid projecting responsibilities that would encourage overstating performance or safety beyond what the text reports.
rationale.RX.3: We must adhere strictly to the scope of projecting responsibilities and not execute edits or analyses of the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should not prompt actions that could mislead readers about the compiler’s applicability or safety, which could have downstream impacts.
rationale.RX.5: We should maintain clear linkage between any future recommendations and specific parts of the provided section to ensure traceability.
